rawOutput:
{
  "topic": "Psoriasis Pathophysiology: A 10-Mark Answer",
  "topic_slug": "psoriasis-pathophysiology-10-mark",
  "level": "Advanced (Post-graduate Dermatology)",
  "goal": "Post-graduate dermatology exam preparation (specifically structured for a 10-mark theory answer)",
  "estimated_total_minutes": 345,
  "modules": [
    {
      "id": 1,
      "title": "Etiology: The Genetic and Environmental Baseline",
      "estimated_minutes": 45,
      "description": "Establishes the foundational 'who and why' of psoriasis. For a PG exam, detailing specific genetic loci and correlating them with exact environmental triggers sets a high-scoring baseline for your answer.",
      "plan": {
        "goal": "Accurately list and explain the genetic loci (PSORS1) and environmental triggers that predispose a patient to psoriasis.",
        "concepts": [
          "Genetic susceptibility in psoriasis",
          "HLA-Cw6 allele",
          "PSORS1 locus on chromosome 6p",
          "IL23R and TNFAIP3 gene mutations",
          "Environmental stressors as initiators",
          "Koebner Phenomenon (trauma-induced)",
          "Streptococcal pharyngitis (guttate psoriasis trigger)",
          "Drug-induced psoriasis (Lithium, Beta-blockers, Antimalarials)",
          "Lifestyle factors (obesity, smoking, alcohol)"
        ],
        "sourceRefs": [
          "Chapter 31: Psoriasis"
        ],
        "prerequisites": []
      },
      "subtopics": [],
      "generated": false,
      "checkpoint": {
        "type": "quiz",
        "num_questions": 5,
        "pass_threshold": 80,
        "question_types": [
          "conceptual",
          "application"
        ]
      }
    },
    {
      "id": 2,
      "title": "The Initiation Phase: Innate Immune Activation",
      "estimated_minutes": 45,
      "description": "Details the exact molecular spark that starts the psoriatic lesion. Examiners look for the specific transition from keratinocyte stress to dendritic cell activation.",
      "plan": {
        "goal": "Map the sequence from keratinocyte injury to the activation of myeloid dendritic cells via LL-37.",
        "concepts": [
          "Keratinocyte stress and injury",
          "Release of self-DNA/RNA",
          "Antimicrobial peptides (AMPs)",
          "LL-37 (Cathelicidin) complex formation",
          "Toll-like receptors (TLR7/9)",
          "Activation of Plasmacytoid Dendritic Cells (pDCs)",
          "Interferon-alpha (IFN-α) secretion",
          "Activation of Myeloid Dendritic Cells (mDCs)"
        ],
        "sourceRefs": [
          "Chapter 31: Psoriasis"
        ],
        "prerequisites": [
          "Environmental stressors as initiators"
        ]
      },
      "subtopics": [],
      "generated": false,
      "checkpoint": {
        "type": "quiz",
        "num_questions": 5,
        "pass_threshold": 80,
        "question_types": [
          "conceptual",
          "true_false"
        ]
      }
    },
    {
      "id": 3,
      "title": "The Maintenance Phase: IL-23/Th17 Axis (The Core Engine)",
      "estimated_minutes": 60,
      "description": "This is the most critical module for a PG student. Mastery of the IL-23/Th17 axis is mandatory for high marks and forms the rational basis for biologic therapies.",
      "plan": {
        "goal": "Differentiate the roles of IL-12 and IL-23, and detail the downstream effector cytokines produced by Th17 and Th22 cells.",
        "concepts": [
          "mDC migration to local lymph nodes",
          "Secretion of IL-12 and IL-23",
          "IL-12 pathway: Naive T-cells to Th1 cells",
          "Th1 effector cytokines (TNF-α, IFN-γ)",
          "IL-23 as the master regulator",
          "Th17 and Th22 cell differentiation and survival",
          "IL-17A and IL-17F functions (neutrophil recruitment, inflammation)",
          "IL-22 function (keratinocyte hyperproliferation)",
          "Clinical correlation: Biologic targets (TNF-α, IL-23, IL-17 inhibitors)"
        ],
        "sourceRefs": [
          "Chapter 31: Psoriasis"
        ],
        "prerequisites": [
          "Activation of Myeloid Dendritic Cells (mDCs)"
        ]
      },
      "subtopics": [],
      "generated": false,
      "checkpoint": {
        "type": "quiz",
        "num_questions": 6,
        "pass_threshold": 80,
        "question_types": [
          "conceptual",
          "application"
        ]
      }
    },
    {
      "id": 4,
      "title": "The Feed-Forward Loop & Keratinocyte Response",
      "estimated_minutes": 45,
      "description": "Explains how psoriasis sustains itself. Framing keratinocytes as 'drivers' rather than just 'victims' of inflammation is a high-level concept that earns extra marks.",
      "plan": {
        "goal": "Explain the autocrine and paracrine feed-forward mechanisms that perpetuate psoriatic plaque formation.",
        "concepts": [
          "Keratinocytes as drivers of inflammation",
          "Shortened keratinocyte cell cycle (28 days to 3-5 days)",
          "Release of chemokines (CXCL8, CXCL1)",
          "Release of cytokines (TNF-α, IL-1) by keratinocytes",
          "Secondary recruitment of neutrophils and T-cells",
          "Vascular changes (angiogenesis, dermal capillary dilation)",
          "The vicious cycle/self-perpetuating inflammatory circuit"
        ],
        "sourceRefs": [
          "Chapter 31: Psoriasis"
        ],
        "prerequisites": [
          "IL-17A and IL-17F functions (neutrophil recruitment, inflammation)",
          "IL-22 function (keratinocyte hyperproliferation)"
        ]
      },
      "subtopics": [],
      "generated": false,
      "checkpoint": {
        "type": "quiz",
        "num_questions": 5,
        "pass_threshold": 80,
        "question_types": [
          "conceptual",
          "application"
        ]
      }
    },
    {
      "id": 5,
      "title": "Clinical & Histological Correlation",
      "estimated_minutes": 60,
      "description": "Connects the molecular pathophysiology directly to what is seen under the microscope and on the patient's skin. Crucial for a complete theory answer.",
      "plan": {
        "goal": "Correlate cellular and molecular changes with specific histological hallmarks and the clinical Auspitz sign.",
        "concepts": [
          "Acanthosis (epidermal thickening)",
          "Parakeratosis (retention of nuclei in stratum corneum)",
          "Munro’s Microabscesses (neutrophil collections)",
          "Elongated rete ridges ('test-tube' appearance)",
          "Thinning of suprapapillary plates",
          "Dilatation and tortuosity of capillary loops",
          "Pathogenesis of the Auspitz Sign (pinpoint bleeding)"
        ],
        "sourceRefs": [
          "Chapter 31: Psoriasis"
        ],
        "prerequisites": [
          "Shortened keratinocyte cell cycle (28 days to 3-5 days)",
          "Secondary recruitment of neutrophils and T-cells",
          "Vascular changes (angiogenesis, dermal capillary dilation)"
        ]
      },
      "subtopics": [],
      "generated": false,
      "checkpoint": {
        "type": "quiz",
        "num_questions": 5,
        "pass_threshold": 80,
        "question_types": [
          "application",
          "true_false"
        ]
      }
    },
    {
      "id": 6,
      "title": "Capstone: Constructing the 10-Mark Exam Answer & Flowchart",
      "estimated_minutes": 90,
      "description": "Translates all learned knowledge into the exact exam format required. You will synthesize the material into a reproducible 10-point structure and a high-yield flowchart.",
      "plan": {
        "goal": "Draft a perfectly structured 10-mark theory answer and draw a comprehensive pathophysiological flowchart from memory.",
        "concepts": [
          "Structuring an exam answer: Introduction, Etiology, Pathogenesis, Histology",
          "Drafting the Pathogenesis Flowchart (Trigger -> Innate -> Adaptive -> Amplification)",
          "Highlighting keywords for examiners (HLA-Cw6, LL-37, IL-23, Th17, Parakeratosis)",
          "Integrating 'Extra Mark' tips (Feed-forward concept, Biologic targets, Auspitz mechanism)",
          "Time-management for a 10-mark question",
          "Active recall and self-grading against a model rubric"
        ],
        "sourceRefs": [
          "Chapter 31: Psoriasis"
        ],
        "prerequisites": [
          "HLA-Cw6 allele",
          "Activation of Myeloid Dendritic Cells (mDCs)",
          "IL-23 as the master regulator",
          "The vicious cycle/self-perpetuating inflammatory circuit",
          "Pathogenesis of the Auspitz Sign (pinpoint bleeding)"
        ]
      },
      "subtopics": [],

        "type": "quiz",

  ]

parsed.goal: Post-graduate dermatology exam preparation (specifically structured for a 10-mark theory answer)

modules.title: Etiology: The Genetic and Environmental Baseline
modules.description: Establishes the foundational 'who and why' of psoriasis. For a PG exam, detailing specific genetic loci and correlating them with exact environmental triggers sets a high-scoring baseline for your answer.

plan.goal: Accurately list and explain the genetic loci (PSORS1) and environmental triggers that predispose a patient to psoriasis.

modules.title: The Initiation Phase: Innate Immune Activation
modules.description: Details the exact molecular spark that starts the psoriatic lesion. Examiners look for the specific transition from keratinocyte stress to dendritic cell activation.

plan.goal: Map the sequence from keratinocyte injury to the activation of myeloid dendritic cells via LL-37.

modules.title: The Maintenance Phase: IL-23/Th17 Axis (The Core Engine)
modules.description: This is the most critical module for a PG student. Mastery of the IL-23/Th17 axis is mandatory for high marks and forms the rational basis for biologic therapies.

plan.goal: Differentiate the roles of IL-12 and IL-23, and detail the downstream effector cytokines produced by Th17 and Th22 cells.
concepts: mDC migration to local lymph nodes
IL-12 pathway: Naive T-cells to Th1 cells
Th17 and Th22 cell differentiation and survival
IL-17A and IL-17F functions (neutrophil recruitment, inflammation)
Clinical correlation: Biologic targets (TNF-α, IL-23, IL-17 inhibitors)

modules.title: The Feed-Forward Loop & Keratinocyte Response
modules.description: Explains how psoriasis sustains itself. Framing keratinocytes as 'drivers' rather than just 'victims' of inflammation is a high-level concept that earns extra marks.

plan.goal: Explain the autocrine and paracrine feed-forward mechanisms that perpetuate psoriatic plaque formation.
concepts: Shortened keratinocyte cell cycle (28 days to 3-5 days)
Release of cytokines (TNF-α, IL-1) by keratinocytes
Secondary recruitment of neutrophils and T-cells
prerequisites: IL-17A and IL-17F functions (neutrophil recruitment, inflammation)

modules.title: Clinical & Histological Correlation
modules.description: Connects the molecular pathophysiology directly to what is seen under the microscope and on the patient's skin. Crucial for a complete theory answer.

plan.goal: Correlate cellular and molecular changes with specific histological hallmarks and the clinical Auspitz sign.
concepts: Parakeratosis (retention of nuclei in stratum corneum)
Dilatation and tortuosity of capillary loops
Pathogenesis of the Auspitz Sign (pinpoint bleeding)
prerequisites: Shortened keratinocyte cell cycle (28 days to 3-5 days)
Secondary recruitment of neutrophils and T-cells

modules.title: Capstone: Constructing the 10-Mark Exam Answer & Flowchart
modules.description: Translates all learned knowledge into the exact exam format required. You will synthesize the material into a reproducible 10-point structure and a high-yield flowchart.

plan.goal: Draft a perfectly structured 10-mark theory answer and draw a comprehensive pathophysiological flowchart from memory.
concepts: Structuring an exam answer: Introduction, Etiology, Pathogenesis, Histology
Drafting the Pathogenesis Flowchart (Trigger -> Innate -> Adaptive -> Amplification)
Highlighting keywords for examiners (HLA-Cw6, LL-37, IL-23, Th17, Parakeratosis)
Integrating 'Extra Mark' tips (Feed-forward concept, Biologic targets, Auspitz mechanism)
Active recall and self-grading against a model rubric
prerequisites: Pathogenesis of the Auspitz Sign (pinpoint bleeding)
